title: ConceptPose: Training-Free Zero-Shot Object Pose Estimation using Concept Vectors
abstract: Object pose estimation is a fundamental task in computer vision and robotics, yet most methods require extensive, dataset-specific training. Concurrently, large-scale vision language models show remarkable zero-shot capabilities. We bridge these two worlds by introducing ConceptPose, a framework for object pose estimation that is both training-free and model-free. ConceptPose leverages a vision-language model (VLM) to create open-vocabulary 3D concept maps, where each point is tagged with a concept vector derived from saliency maps. By establishing robust 3D-3D correspondences across concept maps, our approach allows precise estimation of 6DoF relative pose. Without any object- or dataset-specific training, it achieves state-of-the-art results on common zero-shot relative pose benchmarks, outperforming the strongest baseline by a relative 62% in average ADD(-S).

title: Node-RF: Learning Generalized Continuous Space-Time Scene Dynamics with Neural ODE-based NeRFs
abstract: Predicting scene dynamics from visual observations is challenging. Existing methods capture dynamics only within observed boundaries, failing to extrapolate far beyond the training sequence. Node-RF (Neural ODE-based NeRF) overcomes this by integrating Neural Ordinary Differential Equations (NODEs) with dynamic Neural Radiance Fields (NeRFs), enabling a continuous-time, spatiotemporal representation that generalizes beyond observed trajectories at constant memory cost. From visual input, Node-RF learns an implicit scene state that evolves over time via an ODE solver, propagating feature embeddings via differential calculus. A NeRF-based renderer interprets the embeddings to synthesize arbitrary views for long-range extrapolation. Training on multiple motion sequences with shared dynamics enables generalization to unseen conditions.

title: Object Pose Transformer: Unifying Unseen Object Pose Estimation
abstract: Learning model-free object pose estimation for unseen instances remains a fundamental challenge in 3D vision. Existing methods fall into two disjoint paradigms: category-level approaches predict absolute poses in a canonical space but rely on predefined taxonomies, while relative pose methods estimate cross-view transformations but cannot recover single-view absolute pose. We propose Object Pose Transformer (OPT-Pose), a unified feed-forward framework that bridges these paradigms through task factorization within a single model. OPT-Pose jointly predicts depth, point maps, camera parameters, and normalized object coordinates (NOCS) from RGB inputs, enabling both category-level absolute SA(3) pose and unseen-object relative SE(3) pose. Extensive experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance in both absolute and relative pose estimation within a single architecture.

title: UnReflectAnything: RGB-Only Highlight Removal by Rendering Synthetic Specular Supervision
abstract: Specular highlights distort appearance, obscure texture, and hinder geometric reasoning in both natural and surgical imagery. We present UnReflectAnything, an RGB-only framework that removes highlights from a single image by predicting a highlight map together with a reflection-free diffuse reconstruction. The model uses a frozen vision transformer encoder to extract multi-scale features, a lightweight head to localize specular regions, and a token-level inpainting module that restores corrupted feature patches before producing the final diffuse image. To overcome the lack of paired supervision, we introduce a Virtual Highlight Synthesis pipeline that renders physically plausible specularities using monocular geometry, Fresnel-aware shading, and randomized lighting. The method generalizes across natural and surgical domains and achieves competitive results on several benchmarks.

title: Neural Implicit Surfaces from Oblique Aerial Photogrammetry
abstract: Oblique aerial captures rich facade detail that 2.5D meshes flatten. We fit a neural signed-distance field directly to oblique image sets, recovering watertight building shells with crisp eaves and balconies, and export them to standard mesh formats for downstream GIS use.

title: Change Detection in Multi-Temporal SAR with Transformer Attention
abstract: We model a stack of co-registered SAR acquisitions as a temporal sequence and let attention decide which dates matter for each pixel. The approach flags construction and flooding earlier than bi-temporal differencing, with calibrated uncertainty for operational use.

title: Panoptic Segmentation of Street-Level Mobile-Mapping Point Clouds
venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
abstract: Mobile-mapping clouds mix stuff (road, vegetation) and things (cars, poles, pedestrians). We propose a sparse-convolution panoptic head that resolves instances even in the long, thin geometry of street furniture, and release a re-annotated Munich subset for evaluation.

title: Cross-View Geo-Localisation Between Ground Photos and Satellite Tiles
venue: IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision
abstract: Given a single street photo, where on the map was it taken? We learn a shared embedding across the extreme viewpoint gap between phone photos and satellite imagery, adding an orientation head that recovers heading to within a few degrees.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Canopy Height Mapping from Spaceborne Lidar and Optical Fusion
venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: We fuse sparse spaceborne lidar footprints with dense optical imagery to produce wall-to-wall canopy height, propagating per-pixel uncertainty end to end. The uncertainty maps correlate with field error and let downstream carbon estimates report honest confidence intervals.

title: Gaussian Splatting for Large-Scale Urban Scene Capture
abstract: We scale 3D Gaussian splatting to city blocks by tiling the optimisation and sharing appearance across overlapping captures. The result renders novel views of streets in real time on commodity hardware, and the splats convert cleanly to meshes for measurement.

title: Weakly-Supervised Building Footprint Extraction from Noisy OSM Labels
abstract: OpenStreetMap footprints are plentiful but misaligned. We treat them as weak supervision, learning a segmentation model robust to label shift through an alignment-tolerant loss, and recover sharper footprints than the noisy labels themselves.

title: Temporal Super-Resolution of Sentinel-2 Time Series
abstract: Clouds punch gaps in optical time series. We interpolate the missing acquisitions with a temporal model conditioned on SAR, reconstructing plausible surface reflectance for agricultural monitoring even through extended overcast periods.

title: Learning Robust Local Descriptors for Point-Cloud Registration
abstract: We revisit hand-crafted geometric descriptors through a learned lens, training rotation-equivariant features that stay discriminative under heavy density variation. The descriptors plug into standard RANSAC pipelines and improve recall on cross-source benchmarks.

title: Flood Extent Mapping with Physics-Guided Neural Networks
venue: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: We constrain a flood-mapping network with a coarse hydrological prior so predictions respect terrain flow. The guidance suppresses false positives on dark, smooth surfaces that mimic water in SAR, improving reliability for rapid disaster response.

title: Domain Adaptation Across Cities for Semantic Aerial Mapping
venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
abstract: A model trained on one city degrades on the next as roof materials and street patterns shift. We close the gap with an adversarial alignment of feature statistics plus targeted pseudo-labelling, recovering most of the lost accuracy without new annotations.

title: Differentiable Bundle Adjustment for End-to-End Photogrammetry
abstract: We make the classical bundle-adjustment step differentiable so that feature extraction and geometry optimisation train jointly. Gradients from reprojection error reach the front-end network, yielding features tuned for the optimiser rather than for matching alone.

title: Snow-Cover Monitoring in Alpine Terrain from Fused Optical and SAR
abstract: Steep terrain confounds single-sensor snow mapping through shadow and layover. A fusion model reconciles optical reflectance with SAR backscatter under a terrain-aware geometry, producing daily snow-cover maps validated against in-situ stations.

title: Open-Vocabulary Querying of City-Scale Point Clouds
abstract: We attach language features to points so a user can ask for 'bike racks near the entrance' and get a spatial answer. By distilling a vision-language model into a sparse 3D backbone, the system answers free-form queries over hundreds of millions of points.

title: Photogrammetric Deformation Monitoring of Bridges from Consumer Cameras
venue: ISPRS Annals of the Photogrammetry, Remote Sensing and Spatial Information Sciences
abstract: Sub-millimetre structural monitoring usually needs survey-grade gear. We show that a calibrated cluster of consumer cameras, combined with careful bundle adjustment and temporal averaging, resolves bridge deflection trends at a fraction of the cost.

title: Foundation Models for Earth Observation: A Critical Survey
venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
abstract: Large pretrained models promise general-purpose remote sensing, but claims outpace evidence. We survey pretraining data, architectures, and evaluation, and argue for benchmarks that test geographic generalisation and physical consistency rather than in-distribution accuracy alone.
